description:
## Overview

Restart the device or trigger a firmware update. Both operations interrupt normal device operation and require careful timing.

## Commands

| Command | Description |
|---|---|
| `reboot` | Restart the device and apply pending configuration changes |
| `set_os` | Trigger a firmware update on the device |

## Requirements

- Reboot: No active inventory operation
- Firmware update: Sufficient battery level required
- Both operations apply configuration changes
- Device will reconnect to broker after reboot

post.description:
## Overview

The `reboot` command performs a warm reset of the device. After a successful reboot, the device automatically reinitializes its connection to the previously connected server. If the reboot fails, a failure notification is sent.

Use this command to:
- Restart the device for applying configuration changes
- Recover from error states
- Reinitialize device connections
- Apply pending device updates

---

## Command Details

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Pattern Name | Device Reboot |
| Communication Type | Bidirectional (Cloud to Device, Device to Cloud) |
| Applies To | RFD40 Series, RFD90 Series |

---

## Request Parameters

| Parameter | Description |
|---|---|
| `command` | Specifies the operation to be executed. Must be set to `reboot`. |
| `requestId` | A unique identifier for the request, allowing tracking and debugging of the operation. |

---

## Important Notes

- Upon successful reboot, the device will reconnect to the previously connected server.
- Configuration changes made via `config_events` and `set_operating_mode` require a reboot to take effect.
- The device may be temporarily unavailable during the reboot process.

---

### Related Commands

| Command | Description |
|---|---|
| `config_events` | Configures device events (changes require reboot). |
| `config_endpoint` | Configures data transmission endpoints. |
| `set_operating_mode` | Configures operating mode (changes may require reboot). |

command.description: Command issued to reboot the reader

requestId.description: A unique identifier for the request, allowing tracking and debugging of the operation.

command.description: Echoes the command name so clients can map this response to the reboot request.

requestId.description: Request identifier copied from the command, used for request and response correlation.

apiVersion.description: API version used to interpret this response payload.

response.title: response
response.description: Execution result object containing status code and result description.

code.description:
Response code indicating success or failure.

- 0 — Success
- 5 — Can't reboot device, inventory in progress

description.description: Human-readable result message that explains the response code.

x-error-codes.description: Can't reboot device, inventory in progress
x-error-codes.cause: An RFID inventory operation is currently active
x-error-codes.recommended_action: Stop the inventory with control_operation before rebooting

post.description:
The set_os command starts a firmware update workflow on the reader using a provided firmware source URL and connection/authentication settings.

This command allows you to configure:
- Firmware download source URL
- Authentication mode for firmware access
- TLS verification mode
- Optional authentication credentials, headers, query parameters, and certificate material

Use this command to:
- Roll out firmware upgrades to devices
- Apply maintenance and security firmware releases
- Control how firmware is downloaded and verified

### Command Details

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Pattern Name | Firmware Update |
| Communication Type | Bidirectional (Cloud to Device, Device to Cloud) |
| Applies To | RFD40 Series, RFD90 Series |
| Related Commands | [get_version](#op-get-version), [reboot](#op-reboot), [install_certificate](#op-install-certificate) |
| Required Request Fields | command, requestId, OSUpdateDetails |
| Supported Operations | Start firmware update using URL, authentication, and verification settings |
| Supported Types | Authentication types: NONE, BASIC, TOKEN, CERTIFICATE; verification types: NONE, VERIFY_PEER, VERIFY_HOST, VERIFY_HOST_PEER |
| Supported Profiles | Not applicable |
| Supported Protocols | URI-based firmware source URLs |
| Supported API Versions | V1.0, V1.1 |

command.description: Specifies the operation being performed, in this case, setting the reader operating system.

requestId.description: A unique identifier for the request, allowing tracking and debugging of the operation.

OSUpdateDetails.title: OSUpdateDetails
OSUpdateDetails.description: Object containing the details required for the OS update, including the URL of the firmware, authentication type, verification type, and CA certificate file content if applicable.

url.description: The URL where the OS update can be downloaded. Must be a valid URI.

authenticationType.description: Specifies the type of authentication required for accessing the URL. NONE - No authentication is needed. BASIC - Username and password are required. CERTIFICATE - A valid inline certificate or preinstalled certificate is required. TOKEN - Token-based authentication is required.

authOptions.description: Contains authentication options such as username and password for BASIC authentication.

key.description: The name of the HTTP header parameter.

value.description: The value of the HTTP header parameter.

headerParam.description: A list of custom header parameters to include in the HTTP request.

key.description: The name of the query parameter.

value.description: The value of the query parameter.

queryParam.description: A list of query parameters to include in the URL.

verificationType.description: Specifies the type of verification to perform during the connection. NONE - No verification. VERIFY_PEER - Verify the peer's certificate. VERIFY_HOST - Verify the host's certificate. VERIFY_HOST_PEER - Verify both the host and peer certificates.

caCertificateFileContent.description: The content of the CA certificate in PEM format, required for certificate-based authentication.

caCertificateFile.description: The file path to the CA certificate, required for certificate-based authentication.

Firmware update 1.description: Payload to initiate firmware update with CA certificate content

Firmware update 2.description: Payload to initiate firmware update without CA certificate content

Firmware update 3.description: Payload to initiate firmware update with no authentication

command.description: Echoes the command name so clients can map this response to the set_os request.

requestId.description: Request identifier copied from the command, used for request and response correlation.

apiVersion.description: API version used to interpret this response payload.

response.title: response
response.description: Execution result object containing status code and result description.

code.description:
Response code indicating success or failure.

- 0 — Success
- 1 — Command payload is accepted
- 4 — Firmware update in progress
- 8 — Insufficient flash size
- 9 — File not found
- 13 — Firmware update Failed
- 14 — Battery is low, Cannot update firmware

description.description: Human-readable result message that explains the response code.

x-error-codes.cause: The device accepted the payload and is processing it asynchronously
x-error-codes.recommended_action: Wait for a follow-up event or poll status to confirm completion

x-error-codes.cause: A firmware update is already running on the device
x-error-codes.recommended_action: Wait for the current update to complete before sending another set_os command

x-error-codes.cause: Not enough free flash storage to download the firmware file
x-error-codes.recommended_action: Free up flash space or use a smaller firmware package

x-error-codes.cause: The specified file or certificate could not be found on the device or server
x-error-codes.recommended_action: Verify the file path or URL is correct and the file exists

x-error-codes.cause: The firmware download or installation failed
x-error-codes.recommended_action: Check the firmware URL, network connectivity, and retry the update

x-error-codes.description: Battery is low, Cannot update firmware
x-error-codes.cause: Battery charge is too low to safely perform a firmware update
x-error-codes.recommended_action: Charge the device or connect to external power before retrying
